data.description:
## Context
Discovered while implementing epic sonic-columned-sturgeon (op-certs). Children 4214 (merge-gate
verify), ee0b (trusted-server producer), and 8f1d (HMAC->opcert migration) ALL depend on a shared,
currently-unspecified primitive: **how an asymmetric op-cert (a DSSE envelope) is stored on a
ticket and produced.** The epic decomposition treated this as implicit, but it is a core
event-sourcing change, so it needs its own ticket + a design decision confirmed before build.

Concretely: today attestations are HMAC records. `reducer/_processors.py::process_signature`
builds the `attestations[kind]` record from a FIXED field set (manifest/algorithm/signature/
key_id/head_sha/signed_at/author/kind) — it does NOT preserve an added `envelope`. So storing an
op-cert DSSE envelope requires touching the reducer (which runs on every ticket replay + the
SNAPSHOT/compaction fold path at _processors.py:763) plus adding a producer in signing.py. This is
higher blast radius than any one child and has cross-clone rollout implications (a new event
field/type is preserved-and-ignored by older clones; upgrade reconcile hosts first).

## Design options (pick one — the decision this ticket exists to make)
- **Option A — extend the SIGNATURE record.** Add an optional `envelope` (DSSE dict) + bound
  `{ticket_id, material_fingerprint, merged_log_commit}` to the SIGNATURE event `data`; teach
  `process_signature` (and the compaction fold) to preserve them in `attestations[kind]`. A new
  `signing.sign_opcert_manifest(...)` producer writes such an event using `opcert.sign_opcert`.
  Smallest change; op-cert and HMAC coexist during the expand phase (8f1d removes HMAC later).
- **Option B — a new OPCERT event type + `state['opcerts']` map.** Cleaner separation; larger
  change (new event type, new processor, new state field, rollout entry).

Recommendation: **Option A** (least core-machinery churn; aligns with the expand→contract migration
in 8f1d). Confirm before build given the reducer blast radius.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] A producer stores an op-cert (DSSE envelope from `opcert.sign_opcert`) on a ticket, bound to
      `{ticket_id, material_fingerprint, merged_log_commit}`, via the chosen storage model.
- [ ] The reducer preserves the op-cert envelope + bound fields into the ticket's reduced state
      (and through the SNAPSHOT/compaction fold), with a test asserting replay round-trips it.
- [ ] A read helper returns `(envelope, bound_fields)` for a ticket's op-cert of a given kind, or
      None when absent; older clones preserve-and-ignore the new field/event (rollout test).
- [ ] Documented rollout note (upgrade reconcile hosts first), mirroring the TAG_DELTA precedent.

## Success Criteria
- 4214 can read a stored op-cert to verify it; ee0b/8f1d can produce op-certs onto tickets.
data.title: op-cert storage + producer primitive (DSSE envelope on a ticket) — shared keystone for 4214/ee0b/8f1d
